core_mechanism: short-time parametrix construction for a linear evolution semigroup: an approximate fundamental-solution (propagator kernel) built as a leading short-time asymptotic term plus a controllably small remainder that approximately inverts the generator, then bootstrapped into resolvent and spectral (eigenvalue-counting / pseudospectral) estimates
brief_reason: The abstract builds a short-time parametrix for the Fokker–Planck semigroup and uses this approximate short-time propagator to derive an approximate polar decomposition, a resolvent parametrix, pseudospectral estimates, and eigenvalue-count asymptotics, which is the generic machinery of approximating a linear evolution semigroup's kernel to extract spectral information.
